name: From Colmenar Viejo to the Mirador de Valdelaganar - circular via the Río Manzanares
name_original: De Colmenar Viejo al Mirador de Valdelaganar – circular por el Río Manzanares
region_intro: Hiking around Colmenar Viejo, located north of Madrid at the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The region encompasses parts of the highly protected Regional Park of the Upper Manzanares Basin and the Dehesa de Navalvillar, known for its biodiversity. The terrain features extensive grasslands, areas shaped by traditional granite mining, and prominent natural features like the Cerro de San Pedro and the tranquil Pedrezuela Reservoir. These varied environments provide a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Colmenar Viejo The most popular hiking route is Cerro de San Pedro — round trip via Cerro de la Prestancia, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Cerro de San Pedro. Another top favourite among local hikers is Marmot Bridge – Grajal Bridge loop from Presa del Grajal, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.1 km) path. This route features mostly accessible paths and passes by the Grajal Bridge. Local hikers also love the Grajal Bridge – Batán Bridge loop from Presa del Grajal, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through natural beauty around the historic Batán Bridge, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Colmenar Viejo is defined by its protected natural areas, the backdrop of the Sierra de Guadarrama, and landscapes shaped by grasslands and granite mining. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging routes. The routes in Colmenar Viejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2600 hikers have used komoot to explore Colmenar Viejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Colmenar Viejo offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 50 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to challenging treks, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails around Colmenar Viejo?
A: The terrain in Colmenar Viejo is quite diverse. You'll find extensive grasslands, areas shaped by traditional granite mining, and sections with small hills, rocks, and roots. Some routes, especially those in the Sierra de Guadarrama foothills, can feature steep sections.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Yes, Colmenar Viejo has several easy trails suitable for families. The municipality has developed eight ecological routes, many of which can be adapted for shorter, less strenuous walks. The area around the Hervidero Waterfall, with its small lake, is also a popular spot for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in Colmenar Viejo are dog-friendly, especially those within the Dehesa de Navalvillar and other natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Regional Park of the Upper Manzanares Basin, to protect local wildlife and livestock.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I see while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover several natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter the tranquil Pedrezuela Reservoir, the historic Batán Bridge, or the charming Hervidero Waterfall. The majestic Sierra de Guadarrama provides a stunning backdrop, and you can enjoy panoramic views from the Valdeliganar Viewpoint. Other notable features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2497364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cerro de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; and unique rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/565721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Elefantito&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Colmenar Viejo are designed as circular routes, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5318328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmot Bridge – Grajal Bridge loop from Presa del Grajal&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31745942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grajal Bridge – Batán Bridge loop from Presa del Grajal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Colmenar Viejo offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable, while winters are typically cooler but still suitable for hiking, especially on sunny days.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and within the town of Colmenar Viejo. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route descriptions or local tourism resources.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Colmenar Viejo using public transport?
A: Colmenar Viejo is accessible by public transport from Madrid, and local bus services may connect to areas closer to some trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes for specific access points to the hiking areas.

Q: Do I need any permits to hike in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for hiking on the established trails in Colmenar Viejo. However, a significant portion of the territory is within the Regional Park of the Upper Manzanares Basin, a protected area, so always adhere to local regulations regarding nature conservation.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Colmenar Viejo?
A: The hiking trails in Colmenar Viejo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, Colmenar Viejo offers routes with more significant elevation changes and varied terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957826187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Colmenar Viejo to the Mirador de Valdelaganar - circular via the Río Manzanares&lt;/a&gt;, which demands very good fitness.
tips: Route along a very marked path passing through a stream with stones, seeing the Batán and Grajal bridges ending at the Colmenar Viejo train station.

The history of this picturesque bridge dates back to Andalusian times and was once part of the Cordovan defensive system known as the Middle Mark (IX - XI).
Today we see a remodeling of the original bridge that partially altered its appearance but left its essence unchanged.

Dates from the 18th century. It was ordered to be built, directly, by Ferdinand VI to give continuity to the surveillance of the fence of the Mount of El Pardo, a fence that he also had built to prevent poaching within the mount, a royal hunting reserve.
You can enjoy a panoramic view of the Sierra de Guadarrama and a pleasant silence. There are many routes for all levels.
